WorkJournals.Title: Week 1 — Network Basics
WorkJournals.Content: This week I was introduced to the IT infrastructure at Swisscom. I learned the difference between LAN, WAN and WLAN. Together with my trainer Markus I set up a small test network and configured the IP addresses manually. I also attended an onboarding presentation about company values and data security policies.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 2 — Helpdesk Shadowing
WorkJournals.Content: I spent Monday and Tuesday shadowing the helpdesk team. I observed how tickets are created, prioritised and resolved in ServiceNow. On Wednesday I handled my first ticket independently — a password reset for an employee in Bern. My trainer reviewed my documentation and gave positive feedback. I still need to improve my response time.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 54 — PLC Programming
WorkJournals.Content: In my second year I started working with Siemens S7-1500 PLCs. This week Sandra showed me how to write a basic ladder diagram to control a conveyor belt simulation. I had to debug a faulty timer block which took most of Tuesday afternoon. By Thursday the simulation ran without errors. I documented all steps in the logbook.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 55 — Electrical Panel Wiring
WorkJournals.Content: This week I wired a control cabinet under supervision. I followed the circuit diagram exactly and double-checked every connection before powering on. We measured voltages with a multimeter at all test points — all within tolerance. Sandra confirmed the wiring was correct on first inspection. I am gaining more confidence working with live components.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 110 — Inventory Management Project
WorkJournals.Content: Third year project week. I am leading a small team of two colleagues to analyse the inventory discrepancies in the fresh produce department. We collected data from the ERP system and created an Excel pivot table. The main cause seems to be incorrect scanning during goods receipt. I prepared a short presentation for Thomas and the department manager.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 111 — Customer Service Focus
WorkJournals.Content: This week I was assigned to the customer service desk. I handled approximately 40 customer interactions per day including returns, complaints and general inquiries. One difficult situation involved an angry customer over a billing error — I stayed calm, apologised and escalated to the supervisor as per procedure. Thomas praised my composed behaviour during the debrief.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 163 — Final Year — Client Advisory
WorkJournals.Content: Fourth and final year. This week I conducted three supervised client advisory meetings for savings products. I prepared the client dossiers in advance and used the UBS advisory checklist throughout. In one meeting the client had questions about the current interest rate environment which I answered confidently. Claudia observed and said my communication style has improved greatly since year one.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 164 — Exam Preparation
WorkJournals.Content: This week was dedicated to preparing for the Qualifying Procedure (QV). I reviewed all mandatory competences and filled in any gaps in my portfolio. I wrote two extended case studies on mortgage financing and investment funds. Claudia reviewed both and suggested adding more regulatory context to the mortgage study. I updated it accordingly and submitted the final version.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 53 — Lab Safety & GMP Introduction
WorkJournals.Content: Second year start at Roche. This week we had a two-day refresher on Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and laboratory safety regulations. I passed the mandatory safety test with 96%. Roland introduced me to the new workstation in the quality control lab where I will spend the next three months. I familiarised myself with the HPLC instrument and read the standard operating procedures.

WorkJournals.Title: Week 54 — Sample Preparation
WorkJournals.Content: First practical week in the QC lab. I prepared ten reference samples according to SOP-QC-042. Roland supervised closely for the first five samples then let me work independently. All ten samples were within the accepted concentration range. I documented everything in the lab notebook with timestamps as required by GMP. I feel comfortable in the lab environment and look forward to more independent work.
